item.title: A roadmap for AI, if anyone will listen
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The Pro-Human Declaration was finalized before last week's Pentagon-Anthropic standoff, but the collision of the two events wasn’t lost on anyone involved.

item.title: Push for $40 smartphones builds momentum, but still faces cost hurdles
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: A coalition of telecom operators and device makers is pushing $40 smartphones to bring up to 20 million people online, but rising component costs threaten the plan.

item.title: Google just gave Sundar Pichai a $692M pay package
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Most of it is tied to performance, including new stock incentives linked to Waymo and Wing, its drone delivery venture.

item.title: Grammarly’s ‘expert review’ is just missing the actual experts
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: A recently-added feature in Grammarly purports to improve users’ writing with help from the world's great writers and thinkers — and some tech journalists, too.

item.title: OpenAI hardware exec Caitlin Kalinowski quits in response to Pentagon deal
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Hardware executive Caitlin Kalinowski announced today that in response to OpenAI's controversial agreement with the Department of Defense, she’s resigned from her role leading the company's robotics team.

item.title: PopSockets founder David Barnett talks about building a viral business
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: David Barnett, founder of PopSocket, stops by Equity to talk about scaling the business and what he's learned as a leader.

item.title: OpenAI delays ChatGPT’s ‘adult mode’ again
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The feature, which will give verified adult users access to erotica and other adult content, had already been delayed from December.

item.title: Robinhood’s startup fund stumbles in NYSE debut
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The fund currently offers retail investors exposure to eight startups, including Mercor, Ramp, and Stripe, with plans to expand its portfolio.

item.title: X is testing a new ad format that connects posts with products
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: An ad test on X promotes Musk's Starlink beneath original content.

item.title: Nintendo sues the US government for a refund on tariffs
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: This lawsuit comes after a Supreme Court decision struck down some of the president's sweeping tariffs, which had impacted Nintendo and thousands of other companies.

item.title: Life EV officially owns Rad Power Bikes now
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The Rad Power brand is expected to live on.

item.title: OSHA probing fatality at Rivian warehouse
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: A 61-year-old worker died on Thursday after reportedly getting stuck between a tractor trailer and a loading dock.

item.title: Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort, Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.

item.title: Bill Gates’ TerraPower gets approval to build new nuclear reactor
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: It's the first permit to be issued by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission in nearly a decade.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude found 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox over two weeks
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: In a recent security partnership with Mozilla, Anthropic found 22 separate vulnerabilities in Firefox — 14 of them classified as "high-severity."

item.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned to OpenAI instead, which accepted and then watched ChatGPT uninstalls surge 295%. As the stakes keep rising, the question remains: how much unrestricted […]

item.title: Claude’s consumer growth surge continues after Pentagon deal debacle
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Claude's app is now seeing more new installs than ChatGPT and is growing its daily active users.

item.title: India PC shipments surpass pandemic peak as first-time users upgrade
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Pandemic-driven lockdowns exposed many people in India to PCs for the first time, expanding the country's PC userbase and creating demand for upgrades as those devices age.

item.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

item.title: TriZetto confirms 3.4M people’s health and personal data was stolen during breach
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Health tech giant TriZetto has confirmed that more than 3.4 million people had personal and health information stolen in a 2024 cyberattack, which the company failed to detect for almost a year.
